Two important corrections: (1) middle-right snapshot caption 'Kids - Park - Jim - H.' → 'Chris - Nancy - Jim - 19[??]' (three McEwan siblings at the beach, in birth order); (2) upper-center photo's street-name annotation 'Sussmark' → 'Summerlin St.' (Orlando). The clipping prints 'Monday evening, April 25' though April 25 1948 was a Sunday — flagged this discrepancy editorially. Summerlin St. is the McEwans' first Orlando address (1946-48), later moved to 1129 S. Osceola St.

Concluding page of the Aunt Ellen Porcher / White Hall Dec 1860-Jan 1861 letter cluster (completes pp260-270). Numerous phase-1 misreads fixed: 'Uncle Gaily' (not Bully — pet-name for Gaillard; namesake for toddler Sam = Samuel Gaillard FitzSimons Sr.); 'Mas Tado' (not 'Bro Tade'); 'Mamma took up Mas Tado and Seaman in herlap' (not 'in Marlow'); 'Sam backs up to him' (not 'beats'); 'Thomas can hardly help spoiling them' (not 'spelling'); 'Mame is deep in one of Anthony Trollope's novels' (not 'Mama' — Mame = senior grandmother Ellen Milliken Barker, reading Trollope at White Hall). date_range set to 1860-1861.

Handwritten letter to Aunt Ellen on her retirement from the Charleston Library Society. THREE critical phase-1 unknowns resolved: writer was leaving the next day for 'West Va' (not 'west the' — a curious new FitzSimons-side WV tie); the train was the 'next train to Charleston' (not '[night] train'); '[galaxy]' is actually 'salary' — the retirement resolution let Aunt Ellen keep her full salary plus the honorary-librarian title (matching the 1 Oct 1948 resolution at p501). Letter firmly datable to late Sept / early Oct 1948. Writer = Louise FitzSimons (per p516 pencil docket); most likely Louise de Burian FitzSimons (Theodore Stoney FitzSimons + Salina McCrady's daughter).

Memoir of plantation childhood, formal supper and 'Dad's' storytelling. MAJOR correction: phase-1 misread 'Dad' as 'Had' both times the word appears. The Dad-figure who sat by the fire, called Amy 'Gabey,' and whose funeral filled the yard with mourners is Amy's own father Samuel Gaillard FitzSimons Sr. ('Mister Simons') — not an unnamed 'Had' grandfather. Same funeral picked up on p012 where colored friends come to pay last respects to 'Mister Simons.' Also 'relatives matters' → 'relative matters.'

Aunt Lil teaches the children to dance; measles in Charleston. MAJOR correction: phase-1 'Buenois children' is a misread — the high-res scan clearly says 'Simmons children — who live in the Parsonage' (a clergyman's household). Three named children: Emily Simmons (now Mrs. Charles Russell), Sonnax, and Quillie (now Mrs. Tom White; 'Quitlie' of phase 1 reads as 'Quillie'). Also: 'one of Mauma Mim's dear friends' → 'one of Mam'mie's dear friends' — Mr. Kershaw of St. Michael's knew Amy's mother Mary Anne Perry FitzSimons as a girl in Camden; the friendship is hers, not the Black nurse's.

Charleston / Brookland routine. MAJOR correction: 'Gibson Brown' → 'Jason Brown' (clear J-descender in the cursive) — the Charleston livery operator who picked up rice planters every morning for the Planters Special. Confirmed 'Dad' = S.G. FitzSimons Sr. Confirmed Sasses (Misses Sass) school, Aunt Ellen Porcher, Aunt Louisa, Uncle Theodore (Theodore Gaillard Barker).

Walker family in Washington / Philippi / Pickens farm. Correction: closing line 'The target school near by —' → 'She taught school near by' — Emma Dee Pickens Walker taught school near the Pickens farm on Arnold Run (WV) before her marriage. Writer caret-inserted 'there' above 'She lived [there] until she married.'

Photograph of 'the house that Puck built' + memoir of meeting Puck at Henrietta Halloran's debut dance. Correction: 'He saw each [other] at all the parties' → 'We saw each other at all the parties' (clear W in scan; Amy narrating her own courtship). Writer struck through a false start 'to' before 'debut.' Photograph shows a multi-story brick building with two square flanking towers — consistent with the 1906-08 Charleston building boom (Navy Yard / industrial / depot construction) where Puck worked as a young engineer.

Amy's pencil-ink memoir of the 1926 move to Savannah after Puck's promotion. Three phase-1 misreadings corrected: rented house was on Henry St. (not Drury); elder daughter is 'Ilee' (not 'Iles'); trailing parenthetical '(her[self]?)' was 'Mrs. W.' identifying her mother-in-law as the one who 'never walked' again. Added editorial note clarifying 'Anna' is Puck's sister (Anna Strait Walker Modisette) and Mrs. Walker is Emma Dee Pickens Walker.

Three Savannah-period clippings. Factual phase-1 errors fixed: predecessor at ACL general-superintendent post is R. A. McCranie (not S. A. Hutchings), transferred to Jacksonville (not Washington); Walker's first ACL posting as rodman was Haines City FL (not Adams City VA); Charleston tenure 1 Sept 1915 - 1 Aug 1925 (phase 1 had 1923); Pickens Walker Jr. was Junior Asst. Scoutmaster of Troop No. 18 (not 19). Filled in previously-illegible Ardsley Park body (brick veneer home on Fifty-second St., sold by Mrs. Raymond Harris of John Saxton Wolfe Co.).

Pencil-caption transcription confirmed correct ('She during High school years in Sav. Graduated in 1933!'). Only correction: editorial footnote — Mary Ann Walker's DOB is 8 June 1918 per FAMILY-NOTES + enhanced p629 (not phase-1's 'June 27 1919').

Rock Spring memoir: Becky, Aunt Rosa's wedding. Phase-1 corrections: 'black & tan hair' → 'black straw hats' (the cooter-back-shaped Lowcountry bonnets Becky wore); 'rosh' → 'sash' (wedding dress); 'Brownie' → 'Brownies' (plural; ties into p003 Brownie folklore); 'Mr. Greenwall' → likely Mr. Grimball [uncertain].

Oak Hurst garden during the Civil War. 'spurea' → 'spirea'; struck-through verb sequence 'soldiers ~~came~~ *[were at]* to Oak hurst'; phase-1 garbled 'Cypert bulbs — she covered — Beetles + lily-jonquils' → '[Crypt] bulbs — the loveliest Butter & Eggs, jonquils + snow drops' (Butter and Eggs is a folk flower name).

Amy meets Puck, USC commencement, engagement. MAJOR corrections: 'Aunt Kate's' → 'Uncle Kit's' (= Christopher FitzSimons Jr., Amy's paternal uncle, then in Columbia as district manager of Southern Cotton Oil Co.); 'unsalaried Junior' → 'malarial fever' (the biographical reason Amy went to Brookland to recuperate); 'I.S.O.' → 'so was sent.'

A.C.L. valuation work, Petersburg, Philippi photos. Phase-1 confirmed; minor caption polish. Chronology now ties to enhanced p076's 'fall of 1914' — this winter is 1914-1915, summer 1915 leads into p078.

Saluda summer, Mam'mie's illness, Ilee's birth. THE FLAGGED PAGE — phase-1 was wrong by 3 YEARS on all dates: 'That summer 1918' → '1915'; 'Pickens — Saluda — 1918' → '1915'; 'Ilee was born in Roper Hospital Oct 18th 1918' → '1915.' This confirms the p629 pedigree's 18 Oct 1915 for Emma Dee 'Ilee' Walker. The 'second invalid' is Mam'mie (Mary Anne Perry FitzSimons, Amy's mother — not Pickens). Mam'mie was taken by boat from Cain Island to a Savannah hospital with acute bronchitis. Caption 'Ilee and Mary' confirms Mary as Ilee's Black nurse.
Album page with Mary Ann Walker / Lt. Oswald Beverley McEwan wedding-related material. (Phase-2 by an earlier short-truncated agent; details not separately reported but page is marked enhanced.)

Album page with 7 small snapshots of Robin Corbell at Mt. Vernon (1941-42) and Benning Hills (1944). MAJOR corrections: 'Alice' → 'Ilee' (= Dee Corbell, Robin's mother); 'Browning Mills' → 'Benning Hills' (Columbus GA neighborhood next to Fort Benning); surnames Walker → Corbell throughout (Robin is a Corbell grandchild via Dr. Robert Corbell Jr. + Emma Dee Walker Corbell). Previously-illegible bottom-center caption identified as 'Amy + Pickens with Robin' (compiler + her husband JP 'Pickens' Walker Sr. with their grandson). Center-top photo caption 'Dee + Robin 1941' is written on the photo itself.

Single small snapshot of Chris McEwan in sand pile, Jacksonville. Corrections: street number '3648' → '3657 Richmond St.' (verified at 3x zoom); surname 'Chris Walker' → 'Chris McEwan' (Chris = son of Mary Ann Walker McEwan + Bo McEwan).

Four photographs of Nancy Fletcher McEwan at various ages (1946-1950). Phase 1 confirmed: captions and birth details accurate ('Born in Orange General Hospital — Orlando Fla. Sept. 13th 1946. Taken on 2nd birthday.'). Added note about faint pencil annotation at top-left margin and incomplete 'NANCY — 19' year on middle photo.

McEwan family group June 1951; Ilu Corbell; Jim McEwan 1952. MAJOR corrections: third name in top-caption family list was misread by phase-1 as 'June' — actually 'Jim' (Bo + Mary Ann's toddler son, Hunter's father; b. ~1949-1950 — fits the toddler in arms in June 1951). The first 'June' on line 1 IS the month (June 1951), not a person. Bottom-right caption 'June Iu + Corbett — 1952' → 'Jim McEwan — 1952' (caption written vertically beside a sideways-mounted photo of small smiling boy ~2-3 yrs in armchair, NOT 'a woman seated reading' as phase 1 described). Surname Corbett → Corbell for Ilu. 'Beverley McEwan' → 'Bo McEwan.'

Amy's memoir continuation: Dad buys Shoengaussen, Uncle Kit buys Mt. Hope/Oak hurst/Gibbes, move to Mt. Hope. Major fixes: child with cowbell is 'Bub' (Samuel Gaillard Jr.), not 'Beck-me-ning'; Mt. Hope's new name is 'Willtown' (not 'Welltown' — = Willtown Bluff on the Edisto); 'Oak hurst was the old Barnwell place'; 'Rosemont had belonged to the Gibbes.' 'Dad was to plant there + Uncle Kit [to] finance them' (phase 1 had 'lent Kit & finance him').

Memoir continuation: Mt. Hope ploughed 1893, the Storm of '93, Hunkin's illness. Major fix: the brother sharing the nursery is 'Bub' not 'Bull.' Confirmed: 'Time and dates were reckoned from \93\"' (phase 1 had 'June and Jaks were richaned'). Opening fragment 'rost' likely is the tail of 'frost' carrying over from p005."

Photo page: Amy at Rockville Academy 1899-1901 and at the Sass sisters' school c. age 10. Captions confirmed mostly. Note added: second-line abbreviation in the Rockville caption reads ambiguously as 'Md.' / 'nd.' / 'end.' — flagged uncertain.

Infant photo of James Pickens Walker + photo of his birthplace. MAJOR correction: mother's name is Emma Dee Walker (née Pickens), not 'Louisa Ida Walker' (phase 1 was entirely wrong — confirms FAMILY-NOTES). Father is Samuel Cadwalder Walker (single 'a'). WV place name is 'Overfield' (not 'Crawfield' — the cursive 'O' has a downward tail). Confirms FAMILY-NOTES: 'born 27 April 1883 at Fairfield farm, Overfield WV.'

Four snapshots at Black Mountain NC. Children's key corrected: '5 Nancy Parker' (not 'Nancy Pickens'); position 2 reads as 'Amy' (uncertain — could be 'Mary') Allston; position 3 is 'Pinks.' or 'Pucks.' — a nickname (not 'Pickens' written out, likely JP Walker Jr. as a teen in the mid/late 1920s, since the date_range pre-dates Pucky Corbell b. 1944); last name in the key is 'Ilee' (canonical for Emma Dee Walker Corbell), not 'Iles.' Bottom-photo initials 'A. F. W.' (phase 1 had 'A. J. W.'). date_range widened to 1925-1932.

Newspaper clipping (Jacksonville paper) announcing three debutantes; photo of Mary Ann Walker. Phase 1 confirmed (text is printed and clear). Reordered places: list to put Jacksonville first since article is published there.

Snapshot of family maid Clara Brinson 'Brissy' Williams + envelope of a letter from former Savannah maid 'Edna.' Major corrections: 'moved to Mrs. York [uncertain]' → 'moved to New York'; envelope street '3657 Peachmont St.' → '3657 Richmond St.' (struck through); earlier struck-through line '115 S. Main St.' (was illegible to phase 1); return-address surname 'B. Snowden' (not 'B. Saunders') — so 'Edna' had married a Snowden by 1944. Postmark date: Sep 14 1944. Signature 'A. P.' = Amy Perry.

Bridesmaid-party studio proof for Mary Ann Walker / Oswald Beverley McEwan wedding (May 1941). Studio name corrected: 'Dishinger-Woodward Studios' (phase 1 had 'Dahmer-Woodward'). Same Woodward credited in the p145 clipping ('Photographs of Misses Anthony and Peyton by Woodward') — confirms the firm is a real Jacksonville photographer.

Puck Corbell toddler photos + family at home. Corrections: surname Corbell (not Corbett) throughout. 'Pucky Corbett' caption is actually 'Puck Corbell.' Place name: phase-1 'Berwyn Hills, Georgia' → 'Benning Hills' (Columbus GA, Fort Benning neighborhood). FAMILY-NOTES already documents Benning Hills as the Corbells' 1944-47 residence.

Five-photo album page of Corbell + McEwan grandchildren. Surname Corbell throughout (phase 1 had Corbett/Corbelle inconsistently). 'Robbie Corbett' → 'Robin Corbell.' Middle-right photo: phase 1 hallucinated 'Chris Tut + Susan + [illegible] [an iced turkey?]' describing a girl on porch steps — actual photo shows a boy in a striped t-shirt holding a large wild turkey, captioned 'Chris McEwan & his 1st wild turkey.' Address: '303 Monroe Av' → '303 Norman Rd' (Portsmouth VA). Removed phantom 'Missy McEwan' (Missy is the dog), 'Susan Corbett' (misread).

Clue→One (first horror is the stone jar, not a person named 'Clue'); 'Had when he was in the yard'→'Dad when he was in the yard' (reference is Amy's father Samuel Gaillard FitzSimons Sr.); 'they were made'→'They were made' (capitalized start of sentence)

Major correction — Eulalie Barnwell described as 'a 2nd cousin of Theodore Roosevelt' was a misreading of 'afterwards the 2nd Mrs. Theodore Ravenel'. She married Theodore Ravenel as his second wife — ties this page directly to page 044 where Theodore Ravenel Jr. appears. 'Miss Ivan FleSaussure'→'Miss van deSaussure' (Charleston Huguenot family name). 'shabby Seville ground it'→'Sallie [Seville?] ground it' (a personal name, almost certainly the cook). 'hog had cheese'→'hog head cheese'. 'Andrew Sasson'→'Andrew Larsen' (propagates the enhanced p018 correction). Removed phantom Theodore Roosevelt from people list.

Bud→Bub — caption identifies Amy's youngest brother Samuel Gaillard FitzSimons Jr. (called Bub/Buck in p629 pedigree), not an unidentified 'Bud'. Added Willtown to places (Mount Hope = Willtown Bluff per FAMILY-NOTES). Theodore Ravenel Jr. is the son of the Theodore Ravenel who married Eulalie Barnwell — see enhanced p017.

'Washington Lee & Lee — Varsity Team 1903'→'Washington & Lee — Varsity Team 1902'. Right-margin vertical pencil caption reads cleanly when rotated 90 degrees. Year is 1902 (was 1903). Spurious editorial bracket removed.

Phase 1 confirmed — subject is James Pickens Walker Jr. (Amy's eldest surviving son, b. 5 Jan 1912, family nickname Bo). Caption 'PICKENS — CHARLESTON — S.C.' is the only writing on the page. Added family nickname Bo to title and notes.
Sepia portrait of James Pickens Walker Jr. at Washington & Lee, 1929-1933. Phase-1 caption 'Pickens while at W. & L.' confirmed exactly. Marked enhanced.

Sepia photo of James Pickens Walker Jr. captioned 'Pickens Jr. - 1939-1940 1938-1939 - Richmond St.' (Richmond St. = 3657 Richmond St., Jacksonville per FAMILY-NOTES + p116 memoir). Phase-1 confirmed; added Jacksonville to places.

Photo of Mary Ann Walker as Maid in Revellers Court during her debut year 1938-1939. Phase-1 caption confirmed. Mary Ann would have been age 20 at her debut (DOB 8 Jun 1918). Added Jacksonville to places.

Four photographs of Nancy + Chris McEwan as small children, Orlando 1948-1952. Phase-1 transcription_pass flag finally added (the body had been corrected earlier via p215 cross-reference for 'Sussmark' → 'Summerlin St.'). Multiple photo identifications corrected: phase-1 had confused which child appeared in which photo — Nancy (small girl in dress) and Chris (small boy) both photographed at the same Orlando location with Spanish-moss-draped oak and one-story house in background. The Chris caption has a struck-through phrase between '5 years old' and 'Orlando Fla.' — strike-out too heavy to recover.

Henry letter dated 13 Nov 1960. Two corrections: '4 Birchdale Rd.' → '4 Archdale Rd.' (Charleston residential street); 'a tropical-fish tank ornament' → 'a thermometer for my fish.' Per the p350 Allston/FitzSimons pedigree, Donald McKay Allston III (b. 30 Oct 1948) would have been 12 in 1960 — plausible match for Donald's juvenile hand. JP Walker Sr. died 19 Nov 1960 in Jacksonville — only 6 days after Henry's Nov 13 note, lending the letter a poignant retrospective: JP Sr. and Amy were visiting 'Granny' in Charleston in late 1960, possibly the last documented Walker trip before his death.

Three sepia photos of 'Dee' plus pasted clipping. Phase-1 had 'WAYNESBORO, Ga.' which enhanced reads as 'WAYCROSS, Ga.' (dateline corrected, places list updated). Clipping body now legible: Mrs. Lillian Johnson v. Massachusetts Protective Association, heard before Judge William H. Barrett in U.S. District Court; her husband John 'Cracker' Johnson was a Ware County officer and prominent Waycross businessman thrown from the running board of a liquor-laden car and killed (1934). Upper-left torn paper fragment now partly legible as a school-mural / PTA notice mentioning Mrs. Youmans, president.

Studio portrait of James Pickens 'Bo' Walker Jr. Pencil caption corrected from phase-1 'Picture - Sav. High School' to 'Pickens - Sav. High School' -- the family used 'Pickens' as a short form for him in this album. Small star-shaped pin on right lapel newly noted (likely a school or Scouting pin).

Sepia studio portrait of an Army captain in Medical Corps uniform -- twin captain's bars on garrison cap, U.S. brass on right collar, caduceus on left collar and lapel disk, faint circular shoulder patch at lower right. Phase-1 reading confirmed. Pencil signature/studio mark in lower-right margin remains illegible even at high resolution. Subject is JP Walker Jr. (b. 1912), the compiler's son, in his WWII medical-officer service.

MAJOR correction: surname is 'Corbell' (not 'Cantwell') -- caption now clearly reads 'Robert Lawrence Corbell 3rd - Born Jan. 1940 - St. Lukes Hospital - Richmond. Va.' Matches family context (eldest Corbell grandchild). Final post-Richmond abbreviation, previously read as illegible, is 'Va.' confirming Richmond, Virginia. People list and places list both updated.

Engagement clipping for Mary Ann Walker to Oswald Beverley McEwan (Mar 9, 1941). Multiple corrections: groom's father is 'Dr. and Mrs. John Singer McEwan of Orlando' (phase-1 ghost-name 'John Brisber Beverley McEwan' removed); photographer is 'Dishinger-Woodward' (not Dittinger); Mary Ann was in the '1938-39 Debutante Club' specifically; she belonged to 'the Spinsters and Cotillion Club' (not the 'Symphonia and Children's Club' that phase-1 invented); Orlando law firm correctly read as 'Akerman, Akerman and McEwan' (predecessor of present-day Akerman LLP, founded 1920). Removed Jacksonville and Augusta GA from places list (Augusta Military Academy was at Fort Defiance VA, not Augusta GA), added Lexington VA (Washington and Lee). John Singer McEwan added to people list.

Memoir continuation: corrected 'Charleston queens' to 'Charleston queers' (eccentrics); MAJOR doctor-identification rewrite -- phase-1 'Cousin Gaius Aiken'/'Bob Aiken'/'Glencoe Aiken'/'Mr. Aiken' is actually Cousin Jane (Lesesne Waring) and her husband Dr. John L. Ancrum (the Charleston physician recorded on Amy's 1888 birth registry, book-001 p384); 'Mr. Hunger' corrected to 'Mr. Huger' (Dr. William Harleston Huger, 1854-1906, Charleston physician who took over family care after Ancrum's death and was a great friend of grandfather FitzSimons). Bob and Glencoe in the text are children Ancrum delivered (siblings/cousins of Amy), not the Aikens phase-1 invented.

Medicine-closet/hog-butchering memoir. Two book titles in the medicine closet: 'Household Medicines' and (corrected) 'Till the Dr. Comes' (was 'Tell the Dr. Comes' -- a popular late-19c. domestic medical manual). Minor wording polish; first-line 'best she had for her best loved people' confirmed as continuation of previous-page sentence about Mauma Mim's china and silver.

Two childhood photos of Amy. MAJOR studio-imprint correction: 'Quinby's Studio / 26 King St.' is actually 'Leidloff's Studio / 251 King St.' (gilt script + block lettering visible at high resolution). Pose also corrected: child is STANDING in front of the tasseled chair (not seated), with the mandolin body resting on the seat as she holds the neck. Small left-side photo reinterpreted from high-resolution: subject wears a fancy ruffled dress and wide hat -- looks like an older child/young woman rather than another small-child portrait.

Oval studio portrait dated 1905. Age corrected: Amy was 17 in 1905 (b. 4 Feb 1888), not 'about twenty'. Pose refined: both hands are raised to frame the face (one under chin, one to cheek), not just 'one hand raised to cheek.' Caption ink medium corrected from 'brown ink' to 'pencil'.

Three snapshots at the FitzSimons farm. Top caption corrected 'Sis' to 'Had' (the FitzSimons plantation foreman from Amy's memoir, p016 -- 'Had supervised the cutting up of the hogs'). Middle caption 'Watt' corrected to 'Waite' (cross-referenced with p087 'Pickens + Ilee with the Waite children'). 'Patch' nickname identified via p025 as Amy's younger sister Mary Annie FitzSimons (later Mrs. D. M. Allston Sr.). Caption ink corrected from blue ink to pencil.

School caption corrected: '27 Logan St.' (phase-1) is actually '23 Legare St.' The Sass family residence at 23 Legare Street is documented in Charleston (Sass Alley/Sass Gate); Misses Mary and Janie Sass kept a private school there which Amy Perry FitzSimons attended.
Three-classmates photo at Washington & Lee 1902: middle initial corrected on Pendleton — 'W. J. Pendleton' (phase-1) is actually 'W. G. Pendleton.'
Caption confirmed at high resolution ('Amy Perry Fitz Simons - 1902 + 1904.'); page marked enhanced. No textual corrections.

Buzzie infant memorial page. Bottom-right caption MAJOR correction: 'little Nurse Hooker' (phase-1) is actually 'little Maum Hinda' — 'Maum' is the Lowcountry honorific for a Black family nursemaid; Hinda is her given name. Grandmother caption clarified: 'Mim mie' is Amy's shorthand for her mother 'Mam'mie' (Mary Anne Perry FitzSimons). Buzzie's birth at Roper Hospital 20 Mar 1910 and death at Weldon NC 30 Jun 1911 confirmed.

Cain Island snapshots. MAJOR correction to fish-photo caption: phase-1 read 'Marie Petite + Charles' is actually 'Marie Petite + shark' — Marie Petite is a man's name (holding a large shark by the tail), and 'Charles' was a misread of 'shark'. Middle-row central marsh caption resolved as 'Mim mie →' (Amy's mother) pointing toward the mule-cart photo in the bottom row. 'The Launch' confirmed as caption for the motor-launch photo (written sideways).

Studio portrait of toddler Dee/Emma Dee Walker (b. 18 Oct 1915), Charleston. Date narrowed from 1918-1922 to 1918-1920 (sitter ~3-4 yrs). People entry corrected to canonical Emma Dee Walker. Caption case corrected: 'DEE - Charleston.S.C.' (mixed case, not all caps); dress described as elaborately ruffled rather than tiered.

Walker-Corbell marriage clipping + addressed envelope. Clipping date corrected from October 8, 1937 to October 9, 1937. Envelope addressee corrected: 'Mrs. J. Peckens Walker' restored to 'Mrs. J. Pickens Walker' (phase-1's 'slip of the pen' was itself the misreading -- the writer spelled Pickens correctly). Anna W. Modisette identified as Anna Strait Walker Modisette, J. P. Walker Sr.'s elder sister and compiler's sister-in-law -- not unrelated. Date range expanded to '1937, 1940' (clipping vs. envelope). Hollins College location 'Hollins, Va.' added to clipping body.

Ann Knight Walker wedding photo with bridesmaids list. Names list reread: 'Sis - Mary Ann - Carol - Helen - Anne - Maxine - great aunt Lilly' corrected to 'Ilee - Mary Ann - Carol - Katie - Anne - Maxine - [great aunt? / Goldsmith?] Lilly'. Key correction: first name is Ilee (family nickname for Emma Dee Walker Corbell, the groom's elder sister), not Sis. 'Helen' corrected to 'Katie'. Fourth bridesmaid's preceding modifier ('great aunt' vs. surname) still unclear -- noted as alternative readings. Mary Ann identified as the groom's sister Mary Ann Walker (m. McEwan 14 May 1941, one month before this wedding). Added Ilee Walker and James Pickens Walker Jr. to people.

MAJOR correction: photo caption 'Picture Walter Corbell - Portsmouth 1953' was a phase-1 misreading. The actual caption reads 'Pickens Walker Corbell - Portsmouth 1953' -- it is the boy's FULL NAME, not the word 'Picture' + a (nonexistent) 'Walter Corbell'. The fair-haired boy on crutches with left leg in cast is the compiler's grandson Pickens Walker 'Puck' / 'Pucky' Corbell (b. 13 Nov 1944), shown ~8 months before his death from cancer on 2 Dec 1953 (obit on p227). Cast and crutches almost certainly relate to that disease. People entry updated: Walter Corbell -> Pickens Walker Corbell. Postmark corrected: 9:30 AM -> 10:30 AM, with NYC postal zone 21 added (NEW YORK 21, N.Y.). Stamp identified as Ohio Sesquicentennial commemorative (issued 2 Mar 1953). 303 Norman Rd. Greenacres identified as the Corbell residence in Portsmouth, Va.

Puck obituary + spring 1953 family snapshots. Surname CORBELL confirmed throughout the printed obit clipping (phase-1 had Corbett -- a misreading; FAMILY-NOTES also incorrectly states the obit headline uses Corbett). Age in printed obit is 9 (not 8 as phase-1 transcribed; matches Find A Grave: b. 13 Nov 1944, d. 2 Dec 1953 = age 9). All people entries updated from Corbett to Corbell; Pickens Wallace Corbett -> Pickens Walker Corbell. Upper-photo figures re-identified: a boy + a man + a small child between (the family-notes recollect father Dr. Corbell often photographed with the children). Date-strip identified as Dymo/label-maker-style embossed tape rather than 'hand-typeset.' Cross-link added to p216 photo of Puck on crutches (earlier stage of same illness).

Memoir continuation. Phase-1 misreadings corrected: 'family Mr.' is 'family Dr.' (doctor); 'investrass affairs' is 'business affairs'; 'Had' is 'Dad'; 'enright' is 'bright'; 'his nation' is 'his rations'; 'refused to fish her' is 'refused to feed her.' Removed false Dr. William Thomas Berry attribution -- the man named is simply Thomas Berry. Added Mary Anne Perry FitzSimons (Mam'mie/Minnie) to people list as she is the subject of the closing paragraph.

Phase-1 reversed the school chronology and got the Peabody School years wrong. Caption reads: 'James Pickens Walker -- 1896 -- 1897 -- 1898 / Graded school in Washington D.C. -- / Peabody School -- 1894 -- 1895 -- 1896'. The 1896-1898 years are for the graded school in Washington D.C.; the 1894-1896 years are for Peabody School (a historic D.C. public school on Capitol Hill, attended earlier). Date_range tightened to 1894-1898. Photographer's embossed mount imprint visible above 'WASHINGTON, D.C.'.

Phase-1 listed place as 'Rock Hill, South Carolina' based on the caption text 'Rock Hill Academy.' Enhanced re-read of the embossed photographer's imprint at the lower-right of the cabinet card mount reveals the studio was in 'CLARKSBURG, W.V.' (approximately 'Sharr & Co. -- CLARKSBURG, W.V.'). The 1900 portrait was therefore made in Clarksburg, West Virginia, consistent with JP Walker's family roots in Overfield, W.Va. Place changed to Clarksburg, WV. Caption text unchanged.

Pencil caption confirmed: 'Mary Ann during High school years in Sav.' Date_range widened from 1932-1934 to 1932-1936 to span Mary Ann's full high school years (b. 8 Jun 1918). Notes expanded with descriptive detail of the soft-focus 1930s-style outdoor portrait among wildflowers and goldenrod-like grasses. No transcription errors found.

Confirmed pencil docket on the back identifies the writer as 'Louise FitzSimons' (in a later hand). However, the actual blue-ink signature on the letter itself reads 'Louisa' -- a slight spelling discrepancy preserved in the transcription. Phase-1 had bracketed several words as uncertain; enhanced reading confirms: 'Minnie said, Maggie, Marguerite, Irene & Bo all came to the expense as well [as] Minnie herself.' This is the closing of a late Sept/early Oct 1948 letter to Aunt Ellen FitzSimons (per p515 context). Date_range tightened to 1948. People list reorganized: Louisa FitzSimons (the actual signer) added; Ellen FitzSimons (addressee) added.

Western Union telegram from Pickens Walker at W&L to his mother in Savannah. Phase-1 misreadings corrected: (1) the small printed Western Union promotional sticker at upper-left reads 'This is COMMENCEMENT WEEK' (not 'National Mothers Day Week'); (2) 'WHUPEE FANCY DRESS WONDERFUL' is a separate news item referring to W&L's annual Fancy Dress Ball (a famous winter costume gala, 1929 theme '1001 Nights,' 1930 'Spanish Fiesta'), NOT a description of a 'Whupee fancy-dress swimming team' as phase-1 conflated; (3) 'HG DOINGS' is actually 'BIG DOINGS'. Received-date stamp reads 'FEB 1 PM 7:57' (year not visible). Blue-ink notation '60¢' visible across the address strip (collect charge). Stamped '86' below SAVANNAH GA = receiving office number. Adjacent p131 is a 1 Feb 1933 letter from the same writer on Southern Collegian letterhead about the W&L swim team, so this is from the same Feb 1929-1933 period at W&L (Pickens b. 5 Jan 1912).

Studio portrait of Oswald Beverly McEwan in U.S. Army officer's uniform (Field Artillery crossed-cannon collar discs, 2nd Lt. shoulder bars). Pencilled caption reads 'Oswald Beverly McEwan' -- handwritten spelling uses 'Beverly' (no second e), whereas engraved family announcements elsewhere in the archive (p180, p183, p187, etc.) use 'Beverley.' Body text updated to preserve handwritten spelling; metadata retains the engraved-form spelling 'Beverley' for People-index consistency. Print has a diagonal crease/fold through the upper right. Image c. 1941-42, contemporaneous with his commissioning and his May 1941 marriage to Mary Ann Walker.

MAJOR correction: phase-1 read the caption first line as 'Christopher Gaillard McEwan, Susan born' and added a phantom person 'Susan McEwan' to the people list. The actual caption is continuous: 'Christopher Gaillard McEwan born / June 27th 1942 in Orange General hospital / in Orlando, Fla.' -- 'born' was the trailing word on line 1, not a name. 'Susan McEwan' removed from people list. Chris is Mary Ann Walker McEwan + Oswald Beverley McEwan's eldest child (Hunter's uncle, family context confirms b. 1942). Orange General Hospital was Orlando's 1918 community hospital, renamed Orange Memorial Hospital in 1946; later part of Orlando Health.

Snapshot at Highlands NC + folded newspaper clipping of a lamp/floral tablescape. Caption confirmed as 'Amy / Pickens & / Winnie Barrineau / Highlands / N.C.' (phase-1 had 'Amie [Amy]' -- handwriting actually reads 'Amy'). Added James Pickens Walker Jr. as the canonical for 'Pickens' in people list. The newspaper credit phase-1 reported as 'Mrs. J. P. Walker Jr.' is concealed beneath the right-edge fold of the clipping (only 'by M...' is visible); note added that the attribution is uncertain because Mary Ann Walker had been Mrs. Oswald Beverley McEwan since May 1941. Date range widened from 1945-1955 to 1941-1955. Verso ad fragment described in more detail.

MAJOR corrections (3): (1) Family name in photo caption is CORBELL (matching family canonical and other handwritten records), NOT 'Corbett' as phase-1 read; (2) envelope address is 'Route 2, Box 390' Hendersonville (not 'Box 393'); (3) cancellation slogan reads '1607 JAMESTOWN / BIRTHPLACE OF THE / 350TH ANNIVERSARY' (1957 Jamestown 350th Anniversary commemorative slogan); stamp is the 3-cent Wild Turkey from the 1956 Wildlife Conservation series. Maryview Hospital identified as the Portsmouth, Va. hospital that opened March 1945 (originally Glensheallah Hospital, later Bon Secours Maryview Medical Center). Photo dated Nov 23 1946 likely shows a Corbell grandchild on the lawn of staff housing; Ilee Walker (Mrs. Marcus Latanna Corbell Jr.) added to people list.

Service program participants list, Bishop Allison investiture 1982. Phase-2 enhanced verification: added 4 names missed in phase 1 (Peter Larsen, Albert S. Hoag, W. Benjamin Hutto, William D. Gudger). Existing 19 names re-verified at high resolution; flag added.

Investiture program opening (pp 1-2): prelude music verified — composer 'Tora' on Sonata in F is as printed (unusual; likely a misprint in the original program for Tartini or similar, but preserved verbatim). Veracini and J. S. Bach attributions confirmed; movement names verified.

Investiture program (pp 2-3): Old 100th hymn page. Phase-2 verified the tune attribution on the staff: 'OLD HUNDREDTH / L. BOURGEOIS, 1551' (Louis Bourgeois's 1551 melody, arr. Vaughan Williams). William Kethe 1561 / Thomas Ken 1709 hymn-text attributions confirmed.
Litany of Intercession (pp 4-5): verified; transcription_pass added
Hymn at Procession + Prayers (pp 6-7): verified; transcription_pass added
Sermon + Nicene Creed + Investiture (pp 8-9): verified; transcription_pass added
Seating + Holy Communion (pp 10-11): verified; transcription_pass added
Sanctus + Consecration (pp 12-13): verified; transcription_pass added
Final Blessing + Recessional (pp 14-15): verified; transcription_pass added
Two envelopes to Mrs. J.P. Walker: confirmed pencil annotation 'my 1st letter from Nancy'; lower envelope in pencil not blue ink
Mary Ann Walker at stove, 1941: caption verified verbatim; standardised with transcription_pass field
Infant photo caption: corrected surname Corbett -> Corbell (matches page 183)
Matron-of-honor clipping: corrected name from 'Mrs. Marcus Latanna Corbett Jr.' to 'Mrs. Robert Lawrence Corbell Jr.'; narrowed date to 1941
Wedding invitation: read pencilled '1941' annotation at lower left of album page; narrowed date_range to 1941

Walker promoted to SOUTHERN (not Eastern) Division at Jacksonville; O. H. Page (Owen H. Page) succeeded him on NORTHERN (not Southern) Division at Savannah; Farewell-dinner article hallucinated in phase-1 — re-read full text: Hotel Savannah (not De Soto), silver meat platter+gravy bowl (not loving cup), 26th wedding anniversary detail, full speaker list (Page/Clay/Brogdon/Morgan/Wooten/McNall/Etheridge/Trenholm/Finch/Bouhan/Clarke), presenter David S. Atkinson; out-of-town guests Hillegass(Sumter)/McDaniel/Black

Upper-envelope postmark year is 1948 (not 1944); upper street # 3084 HEDRICK (not 3684); lower-envelope postmark CHARLESTON SC (not Portsmouth VA); marginal note 'my 1st letter from Robin' fully legible; Robin canonical surname Corbell (not Walker)

'Mamie' phase-1 read corrected to 'Minnie' (visible in high-res); confirmed S. G. FitzSimons signature; clarified 'Sis'=Edie per compiler's verso note p654; addressee Hugh = W. Huger FitzSimons (Sam's brother); Aunt Lillian moving from Brookland

Trailing-clause 'but there is bright and cheerful' was a phase-1 hallucination — correct reading is 'but Sam is bright and cheerful' (referring to writer's brother Samuel Gaillard FitzSimons); 'Crofts' School' corrected to 'Crofts School' (no apostrophe in original); confirmed Miners & Merchants Bank

Aunt Ellen letter (Dec 9 1948) p1: 'Smythe ancestors' (not Singleton); 'intermarried with Adgers' (not Africans); 'Dr. Huger' (not Hager) per album-wide list; '(at the Sorbonne)' (not for the); 'Blue Ruin' likelier than 'Blue Resin'; 'gifted as he spoke to me of his pleasure in meeting them' (not 'gifted in his speech & me & his pleasure in writing of them')

Aunt Ellen letter p2: 'when the war was over' (not 'when life was nice over') -- resolves a phase-1 misread to a clear reference to the end of the Franco-Prussian War while her father was in medical school in Paris; 'French Negro woman' (not Creole); 'secured & arranged for' postscript

Philistine letter p1: 'vein of irony' (not 'He being of downy'); 'crumble too much of the joy of life' (not 'if'); 'tend, as Mr. W. P. said to me, to brains & nerves' resolves the [hauns][?] crux; 'vulgar in soul' (not 'vulgarian souls'); 'self-indulgent mainly in a genial way' (not 'enough')

Philistine letter p2: 'than you even suffer from physical causes' restores missing clause; 'making allowances, imagination, sympathy and forbearance' (not 'inequities sympathies'); 'The Hs, those dreadful people' opens new paragraph -- writer identifies herself as one of a Welsh-descended family; 'being one of them' (not 'being one again'); 'said to come from Wales' (not 'well to come')
